title: Full-Time Sales VA — US Cleaning Company (Inbound + Outbound Calls)
description:
Chicago, IL (Remote) | $5/hr USD | Full-Time 40 hrs/week



About Us



We are a premium residential and commercial cleaning company based in Chicago, IL. We service hundreds of clients across Chicagoland — from busy families who need recurring home cleaning to property managers and businesses that need reliable commercial janitorial services. We're growing fast and need a sharp, phone-confident Sales VA to help us convert inbound leads and drive outbound outreach.



About the Role



This is a phone-heavy sales position. You will handle inbound calls from prospects, follow up on quotes, run outbound outreach to commercial leads, and help book walkthroughs and recurring services. You are not a receptionist — you are a closer. You'll be working directly with our operations and sales process, logging activity in our CRM, and hitting weekly booking targets. Our clients are professional and expect professional communication. You should be comfortable talking about pricing, handling objections, and confidently representing a premium brand. If you've worked in a similar service industry — home services, cleaning, landscaping, pest control, property management — you'll ramp up quickly and fit right in.



This role requires someone who is detail-oriented and follows instructions precisely. We run a tight operation, and the difference between a good VA and a great one is doing what the job posting says, not just skimming it. We've built strong systems here, and we need someone who will plug in and execute without hand-holding. If you've read this far, you're already ahead of most applicants — to show attention to detail, include the word "sparkle" as the first word of your application message. We treat every client like a VIP, and we expect the same standard from our team.



Schedule & Compensation



• Hours: Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM US Central Time (with 1-hour lunch)

• Rate: $5.00/hr USD, paid weekly

• Performance bonuses based on bookings and revenue targets

• Health insurance after 6 months



Requirements (All Mandatory)



• Experience in cleaning, home services, or a similar service-based industry

• Excellent spoken English — you will be on the phone with US clients all day

• Proven sales experience — comfortable closing, handling objections, and following up

• Reliable high-speed internet (minimum 25 Mbps), quiet workspace, and headset

• Available 40 hours/week during US Central business hours

• Stable power supply — no frequent outages



How to Apply



 your application to: 

Subject line: Sales VA Application — [Your Name]



Your application MUST include ALL of the following. Applications missing ANY item will not be reviewed:



1. Your application message — introduce yourself and explain why you specifically want to work for a cleaning company (read the full posting carefully before writing this)

2. A 90-second voice recording on Vocaroo (
) answering this prompt: "A business owner calls asking about commercial office cleaning 3 times per week. Pitch them on Melissa & A Mop's services and handle this objection: 'We already have a cleaning company.'" — Share the Vocaroo link in your

3. Describe a specific time you closed a difficult sale over the phone — what was the objection and how did you overcome it?

4. Your internet speed test screenshot from 

5. Confirm your available start date

6. List the CRM tools you have experience with



Applications missing ANY of the above will not be reviewed. We respond to every qualified applicant within 24 hours.

title: E-Commerce Customer Support Representative (Phone and Chat)
description:
About MIRA Safety

MIRA Safety is committed to protecting lives through high-performance safety equipment trusted by civilians, professionals, and first responders worldwide. Our mission is to deliver reliable solutions that help customers stay prepared for the unexpected.

As we continue to grow, we are expanding our support team and seeking experienced professionals who thrive in e-commerce and product-driven support environments, supporting U.S.-based customers.



Role Overview

We are hiring an E-Commerce Customer Support Representative with strong BPO voice experience and proven ability to handle both phone and live chat support simultaneously.

This is not a purely transactional support role. We are looking for individuals who can confidently engage customers, communicate product value, and take full ownership of resolving issues in real time.



This role is ideal for someone who:

-Has experience in e-commerce or product-based support, specifically in Amazon Seller Central

-Can effectively manage phone and live chat concurrently

-Demonstrates high energy, adaptability, and ownership

-Is confident explaining products and guiding customer decisions

-Performs well in fast-paced, KPI-driven environments



This requires a 40-hour weekly commitment remote contract role aligned with U.S. business hours.



Key Responsibilities

-Manage inbound customer interactions across phone and live chat simultaneously

-Assist customers with:

-Product inquiries and recommendations

-Order status, modifications, and issue resolution

-Product troubleshooting and usage guidance

-Deliver engaging, proactive support that builds trust and drives customer confidence

-Accurately document all interactions in CRM systems

-Process and update orders as needed



Meet or exceed performance metrics, including:

-CSAT (Customer Satisfaction)

-First Call Resolution (FCR)

-Average Handle Time (AHT)

-Escalate complex issues appropriately

-Collaborate with fulfillment and sales teams when necessary

-Maintain strong, up-to-date knowledge of products and policies



Required Qualifications

-Minimum 3+ years of BPO voice support experience with U.S. customers

-Proven experience handling high-volume support across phone and chat simultaneously

-Background in e-commerce or product-focused support environments

-Strong spoken English with a neutral accent

-Experience with CRM and support platforms such as:

    Amazon Seller Central    

    Gorgias

    Zendesk

    Salesforce

    Richpanel or similar tools

-Demonstrated ability to:

    Multitask effectively in real time

    Maintain accuracy across concurrent interactions

    Communicate product knowledge clearly and confidently

    Strong problem-solving skills and a customer-first mindset

   Ability to work independently in a remote setup



Work Requirements

-Reliable high-speed internet connection and a back up

-Backup power support or computer

-Quiet, distraction-free workspace

-High-quality headset for calls

-Consistent availability during scheduled shifts



Additional technical requirements will be provided during the hiring process.



Schedule

Weekends+ weekdays, up to 40 hours. Shift time TBD. Schedule may adjust based on business needs



Preferred Qualifications

-Strong product knowledge and ability to recommend solutions confidently

T-rack record of high engagement, responsiveness, and ownership in customer interactions



Why Join MIRA Safety

Application Process

Please complete the form below:

title: Video Editor
description:
We're looking for a sharp, fast-moving Video Editor to join our Paid Media team. 

Your job is straightforward: take raw footage and a storyboard, then turn it into scroll-stopping ad creatives for
, Instagram Reels, and TikTok. 



You understand what makes people pause their thumb, you edit with precision and speed, and you know the difference between content that converts and content that gets skipped.This is a production-focused role. 

You'll receive clear creative direction and storyboards — your superpower is executing at a high level, quickly. 

The ideal candidate lives and breathes short-form social content, has a feel for platform-specific trends, and can layer in effects, transitions, captions, and AI-generated b-roll to elevate every piece.





What You'll Do

-Edit raw footage into polished, platform-ready ad creatives for
, Instagram Reels, and TikTok based on provided storyboards and creative briefs.Crop, reframe, and reformat content to fit each platform's native aspect ratios (9:16, 1:1, 4:5) while maintaining visual quality and storytelling impact.

-Apply clean cuts, smooth transitions, motion graphics, text overlays, captions, and sound design that align with current social media best practices.

-Generate and integrate AI-produced b-roll footage (using tools such as Runway, Pika, Kling, or similar) to fill gaps, add visual variety, and enhance production value.

-Stay current on platform algorithm changes, trending audio, popular editing styles, and ad creative formats that drive performance across paid social channels.

-Deliver fast turnarounds on iterative edits — versioning hooks, swapping CTAs, testing different intros, and producing multiple variations from a single asset.

-Maintain organized project files and a clean handoff workflow so the team can move quickly.





What You 

-Bring2+ years of video editing experience with a strong portfolio of short-form social content (paid ads, Reels, TikToks).Proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro, After Effects, CapCut, or DaVinci Resolve — ideally more than one.

-Hands-on experience with AI video generation tools (Runway, Pika, Kling, Sora, or equivalent) for creating supplemental b-roll and visual assets.

-Deep understanding of what performs on
, Instagram, and TikTok — you follow trends, study top-performing ads, and know why certain hooks and formats work.

-Strong eye for pacing, timing, and visual rhythm — your cuts feel intentional, not choppy.

-Experience with motion graphics, kinetic text, and caption styling for mobile-first content.

-Ability to take direction well and execute against a storyboard quickly and accurately without needing extensive hand-holding.

-Fast, reliable, and comfortable working in a high-volume environment with multiple projects in flight.





Bonus Points

- Experience editing specifically for direct-response or performance marketing campaigns (not just organic content).Familiarity with ad creative testing frameworks (hook variations, CTA swaps, format tests).

-Basic sound design or audio mixing skills.

-Experience with thumbnail design or static creative as a complement to video work.

-Understanding of platform ad specs, safe zones, and delivery requirements across Meta and TikTok Ads Manager.?

s, and other marketing copy

title: Administrative Assistant (Inbound Calls and Scheduling)
description:
We are a growing residential painting company based in Northern Virginia, USA, looking for a detail-oriented, professional Administrative Assistant to join our team.



This is a *long-term, part-time position* with consistent hours and the opportunity for additional hours.



Schedule



* Monday–Friday

* 1:00 PM – 5:00 PM Eastern Time (USA)

* Opportunity for full-time hours during coverage periods (vacations, etc.) and the potential to transition to full-time



What You’ll Be Doing



You will be responsible for handling inbound customer calls and helping guide homeowners through our process professionally and efficiently.



Your responsibilities include:



* Answering inbound calls from homeowners

* Asking structured questions to understand their project

* Determining if the project is a good fit for our company

* Scheduling in-home estimates using our booking system

* Entering and updating information in our CRM (HubSpot)

* Support scheduling, follow-ups, and internal task tracking

What Success Looks Like



* Calls are handled professionally and confidently

* Qualified leads are scheduled correctly

* Unqualified leads are filtered out politely

* CRM notes are clean, accurate, and complete

* Our team saves time because of your organization

* Information is clean, consistent, and easy for the team to use

* You stay organized while handling multiple systems and responsibilities

* You can use critical thinking to handle unforeseen roadblocks



Requirements (IMPORTANT – PLEASE READ)



We are looking for someone who is:



* Fluent in spoken English (clear, confident phone communication is REQUIRED)

* Experienced in customer service, call handling, or receptionist work

* Comfortable following a structured call process (script + judgment)

* Highly organized and detail-oriented

Compensation

IMPORTANT – HOW TO APPLY

Who This IS For

title: Marketing Assistant for Social Media Management, Lead Generation / Nurturing and Client Onboarding
description:
This is a long-term, full-time position for the right person (you will have job security).



If you are looking for a gig or part-time work, this will not be a fit.  I’m looking for someone to work with for years, not weeks.



The hours for this position are currently, Monday to Friday, from 8am to 5pm PST (California time). 



Below you will find details about the position as well as the job duties.  At the bottom will be the exact instructions on how to apply.  I’m looking for someone highly organized and who pays attention.  Please make sure you apply according to the instructions below.



POSITION



I run two businesses, an immigration law practice and a coaching business where I teach solo lawyers about marketing, sales, etc. You will be assisting with both businesses and this is the list of job duties / tasks that I will need your help with on a daily/weekly basis:



1) Create attractive PDFs (i.e guides, checklists, etc.)

2) Video editing (i.e. b-roll, subtitles, etc.)

3) Image editing and creation (i.e. Canva, etc.)

4) Post written and video content on social media (I will provide you the content)

5) Respond to people who comment, react and/or DM

6) Add people as friends/contacts on FB, LI, etc.

7) Research prospects and engage in conversation (online)

8) Return calls to warm prospects (no cold calling)

9) Follow-up with hot prospects and respond to client inquiries

10) Onboard new clients

11)
 outreach to prospects

12) Schedule calls and consultations

13) Create and countersign e-sign documents

14) Review and forward physical mail (pre-scanned as PDFs)

15) Request and follow-up on Google reviews

16) Entire future business / future cases in our calendar

17) Request and follow-up on referrals

18) Organize leads, prospects, clients so we can easily track them





The following are not required but a big plus if you know them . . .



-How to use a Mac (we don’t use PCs)

-Knowledge/Expertise in GHL (Go High Level)

-Experience in information marketing and/or experience working for a business/marketing coach.

-Experience working for an immigration law firm or working with attorneys



HOW TO APPLY (please follow exact instructions)



1) Respond here, on the onlinejobs.ph site, do not send me an

2) Go through the requirements above and, in writing here, let me know about your experience/expertise in each (if you don’t have experience/knowledge in something, that is fine, just let me know)

3) Include a link to a 30-60 second audio that starts with the words, “Good Morning Mr. Kumar” (don’t read a script, just talk to me as if we’re friends and talk to me about whatever you want)

4) Include a link to your OnlineJobs.PH profile

5) Let me know exactly what hourly rate, in USD, you are looking for

6) Tell me what you are looking for in a job and in a boss

7) Tell me a bit about you personally. No business here. What do you enjoy? Where do you life? Do you have a family? What are your hobbies, etc.? This is where I get to know a bit about you before we speak.

8) Include a link to your resume.



AFTER YOU APPLY



Assuming you followed the instructions above, I will acknowledge your application and let you know if you have made it to the next stage. I may also ask you some questions if I need clarification on anything.



Thank you for taking the time to read through this and good luck :)

description:

The Property Management Administrator will support day-to-day administrative and operational functions across leasing, maintenance coordination, resident communication, and basic accounting tasks. This role is essential in keeping property operations organized, documented, and running smoothly. The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, responsive, and comfortable managing multiple workflows in a fast-paced property management environment.



Key Responsibilities

- Coordinate leasing activities, including listing updates, basic marketing tasks, applicant follow-up, and application tracking.

- Prepare lease agreements and ensure proper documentation.

- Communicate with tenants regarding inquiries, updates, and general support.

- Assist with maintenance requests by coordinating vendors, scheduling work, and updating records.

- Track delinquencies and support payment follow-up processes.

- Support move-in and move-out documentation.

- Maintain logs for insurance, compliance, and property records.

- Enter basic accounting data (invoices, payments, updates) as directed.

- Provide general administrative support to property management leadership.



Qualifications

- Experience in property management coordination, leasing support, or administrative operations.

- Strong written and verbal communication skills.

- Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.

- Ability to multitask and prioritize in a fast-paced environment.

- Comfortable using property management or CRM software (AppFolio/LeadSimple experience is a plus but not required).

- Proficient with spreadsheets,
 management, and digital workspace tools.



Nice-to-Haves

- Experience with US-based property management companies.

- Willingness to develop and grow into future leadership opportunities.

- Familiarity with AppFolio, LeadSimple, or similar tools.

- Background supporting maintenance coordination or resident communications.

- Process documentation experience.



Company Overview

At SJA Property Management, we believe success starts with people. Our mission is to create lasting relationships built on trust, respect, and collaboration both with our clients and within our team. We’re proud to set the standard for property management in the Puget Sound region, and we’re looking for passionate professionals who want to make an impact while working in a supportive, team-driven environment.

title: IT Manager / Field Supervisor
description:
Overview

We are seeking a seasoned IT Manager / Field Supervisor to lead our Managed Service Provider (MSP) operations with a strong focus on cloud infrastructure. This role will oversee helpdesk operations, field dispatch, and project management across a diverse client base. The ideal candidate will bring deep expertise in Azure, Microsoft 365, AWS, and hybrid environments, along with a proven ability to lead teams and deliver exceptional IT support in a fast-paced MSP setting.



Key Responsibilities

Lead and manage the MSP helpdesk, ensuring timely resolution of tickets and high client satisfaction

Coordinate and dispatch field technicians for on-site support and project implementations

Oversee cloud-centric IT projects including migrations, deployments, and infrastructure upgrades

Provide hands-on technical leadership in Azure, Microsoft 365, AWS, and hybrid environments

Mentor and manage a team of IT professionals, fostering collaboration and growth

Monitor SLAs and KPIs, and implement process improvements for operational efficiency

Collaborate with clients to assess needs, propose cloud-first solutions, and maintain strong relationships



Required Qualifications

Minimum of 10 years in IT management, with at least 5 years in an MSP helpdesk environment leading teams

Deep technical proficiency in:

Azure AD, Office 365, Intune, SharePoint, MS Exchange Server

AWS services including EC2, S3, IAM, and VPC configurations

Windows Server (on-prem and cloud-hosted)

Google Workspace and Google Cloud infrastructure

Setup and management of Ubiquiti, Cisco, SonicWall, Synology hardware

Strong project management experience in cloud migrations and IT deployments

Proven ability to manage ticket workflows and dispatch field resources effectively

Excellent leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills

Ability to work under pressure and adapt to evolving client needs



Preferred Qualifications

Certifications such as Microsoft Certified: Azure Administrator, AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Cisco CCNA

Experience with MSP tools like RMM and PSA platforms

Familiarity with cybersecurity best practices and compliance standards (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR)



Why Join Us

Lead a dynamic team in a growing MSP with a cloud-first approach

Competitive salary and benefits package

Access to cutting-edge technologies and ongoing professional development

Collaborative and innovative work culture



How to Apply

Please submit your resume, a cover letter detailing your relevant experience, and a short intro video. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled.



Equal Opportunity Employer

We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, or any other protected status.

title: Billing & Procurement Account Manager
description:
About Edge360 / Staff360

Edge360 / Staff360 is a trusted Managed Service Provider (MSP) and Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) agency. We help businesses streamline operations, optimize technology, and scale efficiently by providing reliable IT solutions and outsourcing support.

We’re growing fast and looking for motivated professionals who want to be part of a team that drives digital transformation.



Position Overview

We’re hiring a Billing & Procurement Account Manager who will play a key role in managing billing, procurement, and financial operations. This role requires someone detail-oriented, proactive, and comfortable working in a fast-paced environment.

You’ll be the go-to person for ensuring accuracy in billing, smooth procurement processes, and strong vendor relationships—all while supporting our MSP and BPO projects.



Key Responsibilities

Prepare accurate and competitive quotes for client projects in collaboration with sales and technical teams.

Manage procurement: sourcing, ordering, and tracking hardware, software, and other project resources.

Handle billing and invoicing, ensuring compliance with company policies and client agreements.

Maintain project plans, timelines, and budgets to support efficient delivery.

Act as the main contact for accounts payable/receivable, resolving inquiries quickly.

Work with the accounting team on reconciliations, reporting, and audits.

Manage vendor relationships, negotiate contracts, and identify cost-saving opportunities.

Assist with financial forecasting, budgeting, and process improvements.

Support ad-hoc tasks related to billing, procurement, and project management.



Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, or related field (or equivalent experience).

At least 3 years of experience in billing, procurement, or financial management—preferably in MSP, BPO, or IT services.

Proficiency in financial software (QuickBooks, SAP, etc.) and project management tools (Microsoft Project, Asana, etc.).

Strong knowledge of procurement processes, vendor management, and invoicing best practices.

Excellent communication skills and the ability to work well with both internal teams and external partners.

High attention to detail, strong analytical skills, and problem-solving ability.

Ability to multitask and prioritize in a deadline-driven environment.

Familiarity with MSP/BPO industry standards is a plus.



What We Offer

Competitive salary with performance-based bonuses

Opportunities for career growth and professional development

Flexible remote work options

A collaborative and supportive team environment

Exposure to cutting-edge projects in a growing industry



How to Apply

If this sounds like the right fit for you, send us your resume and a short intro video highlighting your relevant experience.



We look forward to welcoming the right candidate to our team!

title: SEO Specialist Assistant
description:
Company: GWIN HVAC

Location: Old Hickory, TN

Job Type: Full-time (Virtual)

Schedule: 9-5pm Central 

Compensation: $2-4



About GWIN

GWIN HVAC is a Tennessee-based HVAC brand focused on high-efficiency heating and cooling systems, including DIY and Assisted DIY solutions. Its website emphasizes customer support, financing, and a standout warranty position: 12-year parts and compressor coverage plus 7-year labor and refrigerant coverage. The company lists its address in Old Hickory, Tennessee.



Position Summary

GWIN is seeking an SEO Specialist to grow our visibility in search engines, increase qualified website traffic, and help more homeowners and contractors discover our products. This role will focus on improving rankings, organic traffic, and revenue by optimizing product pages, collections, blog content, landing pages, and technical site performance. Because GWIN’s brand messaging centers on Assisted DIY, customer support, and unusually strong warranty coverage, this role will help make sure those differentiators are easy for search engines and customers to find.



Key Responsibilities

Develop and execute SEO strategies to grow organic traffic and non-paid revenue

Perform keyword research for product, category, and blog content opportunities

Optimize on-page SEO elements including titles, meta descriptions, headers, internal links, image alt text, and page structure

Improve rankings for high-intent searches related to mini-splits, HVAC systems, warranties, financing, and Assisted DIY installation

Audit and improve technical SEO, including crawlability, indexing, site speed, mobile usability, duplicate content, and structured data

Create SEO content briefs and collaborate on blog posts, landing pages, and educational content

Analyze competitor websites and search opportunities in the HVAC and home-comfort space

Monitor rankings, organic conversions, and user behavior through analytics and SEO tools

Report monthly on performance, wins, losses, and next-step recommendations

Work closely with leadership, web, and advertising teams to align SEO with broader growth goals

What You’ll Help Us Rank For

This role will help GWIN grow visibility around searches tied to its actual market position, such as DIY mini splits, Assisted DIY HVAC systems, warranty-focused comparisons, and home HVAC buying research. GWIN’s current website and blog already lean into those themes through comparison content, warranty education, and Tennessee/homeowner-oriented articles.



Qualifications

2+ years of SEO experience, preferably in e-commerce, home services, HVAC, or consumer products

Strong understanding of on-page, off-page, content, and technical SEO

Experience with tools such as Google Search Console, Google Analytics, Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, or similar platforms

Ability to translate keyword research into pages that drive traffic and sales

Strong writing/editing instincts and understanding of search intent

Experience optimizing Shopify or other e-commerce platforms is strongly preferred

Comfortable analyzing performance data and making practical recommendations

Detail-oriented, organized, and able to manage multiple SEO priorities at once



Preferred Qualifications

Experience with product-led SEO and collection page optimization

Experience writing or guiding comparison content, FAQ content, and educational blog strategy

Familiarity with schema markup, Core Web Vitals, and conversion-focused SEO

Knowledge of the HVAC, home improvement, or contractor market



What Success Looks Like

Increased rankings for high-intent HVAC and mini-split keywords

Growth in organic traffic and qualified sessions

More revenue from non-paid search

Better visibility for GWIN’s Assisted DIY positioning and warranty advantages

Improved performance of product, collection, and blog pages in search



Why Join GWIN

Join a growing brand with a distinct position in the HVAC market

Help scale a company built around customer support and simplified installation

Work on a site with strong differentiation in warranty and Assisted DIY messaging

Make a direct impact on brand growth and online sales



Requirements: 

High speed internet, capable of having video calls without issues 

Able to share screen during working hours.

title: Google Ads Specialist — Local Legends Marketing
description:
Google Ads Assassin — Build Campaigns That Print Leads for Home Service Businesses



Job Description

We're not looking for someone who can "run Google Ads." We're looking for someone who can make Google Ads the single most profitable channel for every client we touch.

Local Legends Marketing is a fast-growing digital marketing agency that helps home service businesses — roofers, HVAC companies, plumbers, electricians, landscapers, painters, concrete contractors — dominate their local markets. We don't just "manage ads." We build lead generation machines that deliver booked appointments and closed deals.

We need a Google Ads specialist who doesn't just know which buttons to click. We need someone who understands the entire ecosystem — from the keyword a homeowner types at 11pm when their AC dies, to the landing page that converts them, to the conversion tracking that proves the ad actually generated revenue, to the retargeting campaign that brings back the ones who didn't convert the first time.

If you've ever sat in Google Ads at midnight tweaking bids because you KNOW there's another 15% of performance hiding in that account... you're our kind of person.



What You'll Own

Search Campaigns That Actually Convert

This is the bread and butter. You'll build and manage Google Search campaigns for home service businesses across multiple trades. You need to understand that "emergency plumber near me" and "best plumber for kitchen remodel" are completely different humans with completely different intent — and they need completely different campaigns, ad copy, landing pages, and bidding strategies.

What this looks like day-to-day:



Campaign architecture — Building tightly themed campaigns and ad groups. No dumping 200 keywords into one ad group and hoping for the best. We're talking surgical structure: separate campaigns by service line (install vs. repair vs. maintenance), by intent level (emergency vs. planned), and by geography when needed.

Keyword research that goes deep — Not just pulling the obvious terms from Keyword Planner. You'll mine Search Terms Reports religiously, find the long-tail gold, identify the negative keywords that are bleeding budget, and understand seasonal patterns (nobody searches for furnace repair in July... unless they should be).

Ad copy that wins clicks AND converts — Writing compelling headlines and descriptions that speak directly to what the homeowner is feeling. "AC Broken at Midnight? We Answer 24/7" beats "Quality HVAC Services" every single time. You'll run A/B tests constantly, kill underperformers fast, and iterate toward higher CTR and conversion rates.

Bid strategy mastery — You know when to use Manual CPC, Enhanced CPC, Maximize Conversions, Target CPA, and Target ROAS. You understand that the right strategy depends on the account maturity, conversion volume, and data quality — not just what Google recommends by default.

Negative keyword warfare — You'll build and maintain aggressive negative keyword lists that prevent our clients' budgets from being wasted on DIY searches, job seekers, tire-kickers, and irrelevant queries. Reviewing search terms weekly isn't optional — it's religion.



Google Local Service Ads (LSAs)

LSAs are massive for home services — businesses that appear in LSA results get 25-30% more calls than those relying on organic listings alone. You need to understand what drives LSA performance and how to optimize for it:



Profile optimization — Services selected, service areas defined, business hours set, photos uploaded. Every field matters.

Review strategy — You understand that a contractor with 180 reviews averaging 4.9 stars outranks a competitor with 30 reviews at 5.0 stars. Volume AND quality both matter. You'll work with the team to ensure review generation is built into our client workflows.

Budget and bid management — LSA budgets are weekly. Google recommends ~10 leads/week for optimal algorithm performance. You'll manage budgets across trades where CPLs vary: HVAC $45-$85, Plumbing $40-$75, Electrical $35-$70, Roofing $50-$95.

Lead dispute management — You'll rate leads daily using Google's "Rate This Lead" tool to dispute spam, wrong numbers, and out-of-area calls. This directly impacts cost efficiency and must be part of the daily routine (disputes must be filed within 30 days).

Google Verified badge maintenance — As of late 2025, Google retired the "Google Guaranteed" badge and replaced it with the unified "Google Verified" blue checkmark. You'll ensure all clients maintain verification, background checks, and linked Google Business Profiles.



Performance Max (PMax)

PMax is powerful for home service businesses with strong visual assets — before-and-after photos, project portfolios, video testimonials. Average CPLs for home services on PMax ($72 for HVAC/plumbing) can be nearly half what non-branded search costs ($149).

You'll need to know:



How to structure asset groups with the right mix of headlines, descriptions, images, and videos

How to feed Google's AI proper audience signals (Customer Match lists, website visitors, custom segments) so it targets the right homeowners — not random traffic

When PMax makes sense vs. when it's a budget black hole (hint: it depends on conversion data quality and creative assets)

How to read PMax reporting (which is notoriously opaque) and actually extract actionable insights



Conversion Tracking That Proves ROI

This is where most Google Ads people fall apart — and where you'll shine. You need to be obsessed with tracking because our clients are spending real money and they want to know exactly what they got for it.

Online Conversion Tracking:



Google Ads conversion tags on all form submissions, phone calls, and chat interactions

Google Analytics 4 (GA4) integration with properly configured events and goals

Google Tag Manager setup and management — triggers, tags, variables, data layers

Call tracking integration (we use WhatConverts and/or GHL's built-in tracking)

Cross-domain tracking when clients have multiple properties



Offline Conversion Tracking (This is the big one):

Our clients' sales don't close on a website. Someone clicks an ad, calls the business, books an appointment, gets an estimate, and either signs or doesn't — days or weeks later. You need to track that entire journey.



GCLID capture and storage — When a lead comes in from Google Ads, the GCLID must be captured and stored in GoHighLevel (our CRM) on the contact record

Offline conversion upload — When that lead moves through the pipeline (appointment set ? estimate given ? job sold), those conversion events get sent back to Google Ads so the algorithm knows which clicks are actually generating revenue — not just form fills

GHL ? Google Ads integration — Using GoHighLevel's native Ad Manager or workflow actions to push offline conversion data back to Google

Value-based bidding — Sending actual revenue values back to Google so the algorithm can optimize for profit, not just lead volume. A $15,000 roof replacement and a $200 drain cleaning are not equal conversions.



This is what separates a good PPC manager from a great one. When you can tell Google "this click turned into $12,000 in revenue," the algorithm gets exponentially smarter at finding more clicks like that.

Retargeting & Remarketing

Most homeowners don't convert on the first visit. They're comparing 3-4 contractors. Your retargeting strategy brings them back:



RLSA (Remarketing Lists for Search Ads) — Bidding higher when someone who already visited our client's site searches again. This is incredibly powerful for home services because they're actively searching AND they already know the brand.

Display remarketing — Following website visitors across the Google Display Network with branded ads. Especially effective for high-ticket services (roofing, windows, remodeling) where the decision cycle is longer.

YouTube remarketing — Re-engaging website visitors with video ads. Before-and-after project videos, customer testimonials, "why choose us" content.

Customer Match — Uploading
 lists from the CRM to target past customers with seasonal offers (annual HVAC tune-ups, gutter cleaning, etc.) and to build lookalike audiences.

First-party data strategy — In 2026, cookie-based tracking is dying. You need to think in terms of first-party data: Customer Match lists, CRM audience segments, and clean data signals fed into Performance Max. The specialists who master first-party data win. Period.



Landing Pages That Convert

You don't just send traffic — you care deeply about where it goes. You understand that a great campaign with a terrible landing page is a waste of money.



Message match — The landing page headline must mirror the ad copy and the keyword intent. If someone searched "emergency AC repair [city]," they better land on a page about emergency AC repair in that city — not a generic homepage.

Speed obsession — You know that every second of load time kills conversions. You'll flag slow pages and push for fixes.

Mobile-first thinking — 70%+ of home service searches happen on mobile. Click-to-call buttons, short forms, fast load times, thumb-friendly design.

Conversion rate optimization — You'll track landing page performance, run A/B tests on headlines/CTAs/forms, and continuously push conversion rates higher. You know that improving a landing page from 5% to 8% conversion rate is the same as getting 60% more leads for free.

Quality Score impact — You understand that landing page experience is one of three Quality Score components, and higher Quality Scores mean lower CPCs and better ad positions. Ads with "above average" landing page experience see 87% higher CTR and 750% higher conversion rates.



Reporting & Client Communication

You'll build reports that our clients actually understand and get excited about — not walls of numbers that make their eyes glaze over.



Weekly/monthly performance reports showing spend, leads, cost per lead, conversion rate, and ROAS — broken down by campaign, service line, and lead source

Full funnel reporting — Not just "you got 50 leads." We're talking: 50 leads ? 35 appointments ? 28 estimates given ? 14 sold ? $187,000 revenue generated. That's what the client cares about.

Source attribution — Which campaigns, keywords, and ad groups are driving actual revenue vs. just clicks

Competitive insights — Auction insights, competitor ad monitoring, market trend analysis

Proactive recommendations — Don't just report what happened. Tell the client what we're doing next and why.





Who You Are

Google Ads Veteran — You have deep, hands-on experience building and managing Google Ads campaigns. Search, Display, YouTube, Performance Max, LSAs — you've done it all. You can navigate the platform blindfolded and you stay current on every update Google ships (and they ship a lot).

Home Services Native — You've run ads for contractors, HVAC companies, plumbers, roofers, or similar trades. You understand the sales cycle, the seasonal patterns, the high-intent keywords, and the fact that a homeowner with a flooded basement at 2am is the most valuable click in the entire account.

Tracking Obsessive — You're the person who won't launch a campaign until every conversion action is firing correctly. GTM, GA4, GCLID capture, offline conversions, call tracking — you treat tracking like the foundation it is. If it's not tracked, it didn't happen.

Data-Driven Decision Maker — You don't guess. You look at the data, form a hypothesis, test it, and iterate. You know your way around pivot tables, you can calculate ROAS in your sleep, and you make budget allocation decisions based on numbers — not gut feelings.

Retargeting Strategist — You think beyond the first click. You build remarketing audiences, RLSA campaigns, Customer Match lists, and multi-touch attribution models that capture value from the full customer journey — not just the last click.

Landing Page Thinker — You understand that your job doesn't end when someone clicks the ad. You care about where traffic lands, you push for better pages, and you test relentlessly to improve conversion rates.

Ad Copy Craftsperson — You write ads that stop the scroll and earn the click. You understand emotional triggers for homeowners (urgency, trust, fear of overpaying, fear of getting scammed) and you write copy that speaks to those emotions while maintaining professionalism.

Budget Surgeon — You know how to stretch $2,000/month into maximum leads for a small plumber AND how to scale a $30,000/month roofing account without blowing efficiency. You reallocate spend based on performance, not assumptions.



Requirements



3+ years of hands-on Google Ads campaign management (not just boosted posts or basic campaigns — real search, display, PMax, and LSA management)

Proven experience running Google Ads for home service or contractor businesses

Expert-level knowledge of conversion tracking: Google Tag Manager, GA4, GCLID, offline conversion uploads

Experience with remarketing/retargeting: RLSA, Display remarketing, Customer Match, YouTube

Experience with Google Local Service Ads setup and optimization

Strong understanding of landing page optimization and its impact on Quality Score and conversion rates

Ability to write compelling ad copy and run structured A/B tests

Experience building client-facing performance reports with full-funnel attribution

Strong English communication skills (written and spoken)

Reliable internet and professional remote work setup

Available full-time during US business hours (or significant overlap)



Bonus Points:



Google Ads certification (Search, Display, Video, Shopping, or Measurement)

Experience with GoHighLevel CRM and its Google Ads integration (offline conversion tracking via GHL workflows)

Experience with WhatConverts or similar call tracking / attribution platforms

Experience with Performance Max campaign optimization for lead generation

Knowledge of competitor analysis tools (SpyFu, SEMrush, Auction Insights)

Experience managing ad spend across multiple client accounts (MCC/Manager account)

Basic understanding of SEO (you'll work alongside our SEO team and need to speak the same language)

Experience with landing page builders (Unbounce, Instapage, GHL funnels, WordPress)





What We Offer



Full-time position with a growing agency that's serious about paid media

A portfolio of home service clients across multiple trades — you'll never be bored running the same campaign over and over

Real ownership of ad accounts — you make the calls, you drive the strategy

Direct access to the agency owner who values data and results over vanity metrics

A team that includes SEO specialists, content creators, web developers, and a GHL/CRM specialist — you're not on an island

Long-term role with growth potential as we scale our client base

Competitive salary based on experience





How to Apply

If your application looks like it was copy-pasted to 50 jobs, we'll know. And we'll skip it.

Here's exactly what we want to see:



A short intro — Who are you, and why does running Google Ads for home service businesses fire you up? (2-3 sentences.)

Your Google Ads track record — Pick 1-2 accounts you've managed (home services preferred). Tell us: What was the monthly spend? What campaigns did you run? What were the results (CPL, conversion rate, ROAS)? How did you improve performance over time? Be specific with numbers.

A Loom video or screenshots (STRONGLY preferred) — Walk us through a Google Ads account you've managed. Show us the campaign structure, the conversion tracking setup, the search terms you're mining, the remarketing audiences you've built. Show us you actually live in these accounts. This is the #1 way to stand out.

A tracking scenario — Answer this in 2-3 sentences: A roofing client says "I'm spending $5,000/month on Google Ads but I don't know if it's working." You log in and see they have zero offline conversion tracking, no GCLID capture, and their only conversion action is a generic "page view." What are the first three things you fix?

Your availability and expected salary — Be upfront. No games.



Applications without a Loom/screenshot (#3) or the tracking scenario (#4) will be deprioritized. We want proof and problem-solving, not resumes full of buzzwords.



Quick Reference: What a Google Ads Master Knows

So there's no confusion about the level we're hiring at, here's the knowledge we expect:

Campaign Types: Search, Display, YouTube/Video, Performance Max, Local Service Ads, Demand Gen, Discovery — you know when to use each and why.

Bidding Strategies: Manual CPC, Enhanced CPC, Maximize Clicks, Maximize Conversions, Target CPA, Target ROAS — you know the tradeoffs and when each makes sense based on data maturity.

Tracking Stack: Google Tag Manager, GA4, Google Ads conversion tags, GCLID/GBRAID/WBRAID capture, offline conversion imports, Enhanced Conversions, Consent Mode, server-side tagging concepts.

Audience Strategy: In-market audiences, custom audiences, Customer Match, website visitor segments, lookalike/similar audiences, RLSA, audience exclusions.

Quality Score: You understand the three components (expected CTR, ad relevance, landing page experience), how they interact, and how to systematically improve each one.

Attribution Models: Last-click, first-click, linear, time-decay, data-driven — you know which model tells the most accurate story for home services and how attribution impacts bidding.

Reporting Tools: Google Ads reporting, GA4 explorations, Looker Studio (Data Studio), Google Ads Scripts for automated alerts and reports.

Competitive Intelligence: Auction Insights, ad previews, competitor landing page analysis, SpyFu/SEMrush for keyword gaps.

Industry Benchmarks: You know that average CPLs for home services range from $35-$95 depending on the trade, that 74% of contractor calls go unanswered (and that speed-to-lead is everything), and that conversion rates above 8% mean you're doing something right.



Local Legends Marketing — We turn local businesses into the names everyone calls first. Now we need the ads specialist who makes the phone ring.

Show us what you've got.

title: Real Estate Marketing & Admin Assistant (KK)
description:
We are looking for a full-time Marketing & Admin Assistant to support a growing real estate team with marketing, social media, client communication, listing preparation, and administrative support. This is a long-term position with opportunity for growth.



Schedule & Pay:



Part Time - 25 hours per week

Monday - Friday 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM PST

Starting pay: $7 per hour

Opportunity for raises based on performance



This role will support the team in several areas including marketing, content creation, client communication, client follow up, listing coordination, and general admin support.



The Marketing & Admin Assistant will help manage and improve online presence, assist with social media and video content, help create newsletters and marketing materials, and support ongoing communication with past clients and current clients.



This role will also assist behind the scenes with listing preparation, scheduling vendors, organizing files and paperwork, tracking signs and lockboxes, and helping keep transactions and projects organized and on schedule.



We are looking for someone who is organized, proactive, detail-oriented, and comfortable handling a variety of marketing, administrative, and coordination tasks.  



Software & Tools:



Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive, Calendar)

Canva

CapCut

YouTube

Follow Up Boss (newsletters)

Instagram,
, TikTok, LInked In

ChatGPT or AI tools



We Are Looking For Someone Who Is:



Organized and detail-oriented

Strong communication skills

Good with social media and marketing

Comfortable editing videos

Proactive and reliable

Able to work independently and follow instructions

Interested in a long-term position

title: Video editor for Ecommerce
description:
We're looking for one skilled video editor to join our team and create short-form video ads for e-commerce products. Most of the work involves AI-powered video generation tools, so experience with tools like HiggsField, Runway, VEO, Kling, or similar is a big plus.



This is an ongoing role, not a one-time project.





WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING



- Creating short-form video ads for e-commerce products

- Working with AI video generation tools as part of your daily workflow

- Editing and polishing AI-generated outputs into finished creatives

- Iterating on concepts based on feedback and performance data

- Adapting winning ad formats to new products and angles





WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



- Experience editing short-form video content (ads, reels, TikToks, etc.)

- Familiarity with AI video/image generation tools

- Strong attention to detail

- Ability to problem-solve independently — you'll be given goals, not step-by-step instructions

- Reliable internet and availability during EST hours (11 AM - 9 PM Eastern Standard time)





HOW TO APPLY



Please include ALL of the following in your application. Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.



1. Start your message with the word "sunrise"



2. Record a short Loom video (2 minutes max) where you:

- Show one video you've edited or created (screen share it)

- Briefly explain what you made and what tools you used

- Tell us one thing you would do differently if you made it again today

(If you don't have a Loom account, a Google Drive link to a screen recording works too.)



3. Answer this question in your application message:

What AI tools have you used for video or image creation, and what did you like or dislike about each one?



4. Include a link to your portfolio or samples of your work (Google Drive, YouTube, Vimeo, etc.)





WHAT HAPPENS NEXT



- We review applications and select a small number of candidates

- Selected candidates will receive a short paid test task ($30 USD)

- Based on the test results, we hire one editor for the ongoing role





COMPENSATION



Starting at $300/month, with room to grow past $
/month if you can handle the volume and put in hard work!

title: We’re Hiring: Expert Video Ad Creator for Meta (Facebook & Instagram)
description:
About Us:

Our company is a Los Angeles–based design and build company with over 9 years of experience serving homeowners across Southern California. We specialize in ADU construction, home remodeling, additions, and full design-build projects — and we’re growing fast!



We’re looking for a creative and skilled Video Ad Creator who can produce scroll-stopping, high-converting Meta video ads that highlight our services and attract new clients.



Your Main Role:

You’ll be responsible for creating engaging video ads for Meta platforms —
 and Instagram.

Each video should tell a story, educate viewers, and motivate them to call or message us for our construction and ADU services.



What You’ll Do:

Create high-quality, engaging, and persuasive video ads optimized for Meta.

Edit and repurpose existing footage, photos, and renders into dynamic ad content.

Write or follow compelling ad scripts that drive viewer action (calls or form submissions).

Add text overlays, motion graphics, captions, and smooth transitions.

Stay updated with Meta ad trends and optimize creative formats for better CTR and conversions.

Collaborate with our marketing team to align visuals with campaigns and messaging.

Manage and organize project files for easy access and revisions.



Requirements:

Proven experience creating Meta 
 & Instagram) video ads — please provide examples or portfolio links.

Strong understanding of ad storytelling, pacing, and emotional triggers.

killed in tools like CapCut, Premiere Pro, After Effects, Canva, or similar AI video software (e.g., InVideo, Pictory, Vizard).

Ability to design videos in 9:16 and 1:1 formats, optimized for Reels and feeds.

Good English communication and script comprehension.

Able to work independently with minimal supervision and deliver on deadlines.



Nice to Have (Bonus):

Experience in the construction, home design, or real estate industry

Familiarity with AI tools for video generation and voiceover

Understanding of Meta Ads Manager performance metrics and creative testing



Work Hours & Compensation:

Flexible schedule (prefer availability during Pacific Time business hours for communication)

Competitive pay – depends on experience and portfolio quality

Long-term position for the right candidate



How to Apply:

Please send your portfolio or sample Meta ads you’ve created. 



Send your application with the subject line:

“Meta Video Ad Creator – [Your Name]”

description:
We are currently looking to add to our growing team. We are a USA-based takeoff & estimating service company. This position is for remote in-house estimators/Quality Control Reps. Please read the job details and respond accordingly to our questions to be considered. Thank you!



We are looking for Immediate Hire:



Position 1: MEP Estimators like Mechanical/HVAC package, electrical, and plumbing.





Job Details-You will be performing takeoffs and some quality control tasks

title: Analytics & Data Specialist — Local Legends Marketing
description:
GA4, Data & Reporting Wizard — Be the Person Who Knows Where Every Lead Came From and What It's Worth



Job Description

In our agency, data doesn't just inform decisions. Data IS the decision.

Every dollar our clients spend on marketing needs to be traceable. Not "we think Google Ads generated some leads." We're talking: "Google Ads generated 47 leads from 3 campaigns, 31 became appointments, 19 got estimates, 11 closed, and they generated $143,000 in revenue — and here's the exact keyword that drove the highest-value jobs." THAT level of clarity.

Local Legends Marketing is a growing digital marketing agency that helps home service businesses — roofers, HVAC companies, plumbers, electricians, landscapers — dominate their local markets. We run Google Ads, build websites, manage SEO, and generate leads across multiple channels for every client. But none of that matters if we can't prove what's working, what's not, and where to put the next dollar.

We need an analytics and data specialist who is obsessed with measurement. Someone who builds the tracking infrastructure from the ground up, connects every data source into one unified view, and creates dashboards so clean that our clients actually look forward to their reports.

If the phrase "the data layer isn't firing correctly" makes your heart rate spike — and you immediately know how to fix it — this job was written for you.



What You'll Own

Google Analytics 4 (GA4) — The Foundation of Everything

GA4 is our primary web analytics platform across all client properties. You won't just "have access" to GA4 — you'll be the one who sets it up, configures it, and makes it actually useful. Most GA4 installations we inherit from clients are a mess: default setup, no custom events, no conversions configured, no audiences built. You'll fix all of that.

What this looks like:



Property setup and configuration — Creating and configuring GA4 properties for every client. Data streams, measurement IDs, cross-domain tracking, internal traffic filters, referral exclusions, data retention settings — all done right from day one.

Event tracking architecture — Designing the event taxonomy for home service websites. Not just page views. We're talking: form submissions (by form type — contact form vs. estimate request vs. schedule service), phone call clicks, chat initiations, scroll depth, video plays, CTA button clicks, PDF downloads. Every meaningful user action gets tracked.

Conversion configuration — Setting up key events (conversions) that align with actual business outcomes. For our clients, that means form fills, phone calls, appointment bookings, and chat leads. You'll also configure conversion values when applicable.

Custom dimensions and metrics — Building custom dimensions for things like lead source, service type, landing page category, and campaign UTM parameters — so we can slice the data in ways that actually answer business questions.

Explorations and custom reports — Going beyond the standard reports. Funnel explorations showing where users drop off before converting. Path explorations showing common user journeys. Cohort analysis for retargeting audiences. Free-form explorations for deep dives when a client asks "why did leads drop 30% last week?"

Audience building — Creating audiences for remarketing: website visitors who didn't convert, visitors who viewed specific service pages, visitors who started but didn't complete a form, past converters for upsell/cross-sell campaigns. These audiences feed directly into Google Ads and other platforms.

Predictive metrics and anomaly detection — Leveraging GA4's built-in machine learning to surface anomalies (sudden traffic drops, conversion rate changes) and predictive audiences (users likely to convert in the next 7 days).

Data hygiene — Regularly auditing GA4 properties to ensure data quality: filtering bot traffic, excluding internal IPs, verifying event parameters are firing correctly, fixing broken tracking before it corrupts weeks of data.



Google Tag Manager (GTM) — The Engine Room

You're the GTM architect. Every tag, trigger, and variable across every client's website runs through your hands.

Core responsibilities:



Container setup and organization — Clean, well-documented GTM containers with logical naming conventions, folder structures, and version notes. No spaghetti containers where nobody knows what fires when.

Tag implementation — Google Ads conversion tags, GA4 event tags, Meta/Facebook pixels, Microsoft Ads UET tags, call tracking scripts (WhatConverts, CallRail, or GHL's built-in tracking), chat widget scripts, and any third-party marketing pixels our clients need.

Custom event tracking — Building custom triggers for form submissions (across multiple form types and builders: Gravity Forms, Contact Form 7, GHL forms, Typeform, etc.), phone number clicks, scroll milestones, video engagement, file downloads, and dynamic elements.

Data layer implementation — Working with our dev team to push structured data into the data layer for cleaner, more reliable tracking. You understand dataLayer.push(), you can spec out exactly what data points need to be pushed and when, and you can troubleshoot when events aren't appearing.

Server-side tagging — This is a 2026 must-have. With browser privacy restrictions killing client-side cookies, you understand server-side GTM containers, how to route data through a first-party endpoint, and why this is the future-proof approach to tracking. Setting up sGTM containers, configuring the GA4 client, and routing conversion data server-side.

Consent Mode v2 — You understand Google's Consent Mode, how it interacts with cookie consent banners, and how to configure tags to respect user consent choices while still capturing modeled conversions. This isn't optional anymore — it's required for compliance and data accuracy.

Cross-domain tracking — For clients with multiple domains or subdomains (main site + landing pages + booking pages), you ensure seamless session tracking across properties.

QA and debugging — You live in GTM's Preview/Debug mode. You use the GA4 DebugView. You check the network tab. You verify every tag fires on the correct trigger with the correct parameters before publishing. No "publish and pray."



Google Looker Studio — Dashboards That Tell a Story

Reports are how we prove our value to clients. You'll build Looker Studio dashboards that are so clear and compelling that clients forward them to their business partners.

What you'll build:



Client-facing performance dashboards — Monthly/weekly views showing: total leads (by source), cost per lead, conversion rates, phone calls vs. form fills vs. chats, top-performing landing pages, top-performing keywords/campaigns, and trend lines over time.

Full-funnel reporting — This is the holy grail. Connecting the top of funnel (ad clicks, website traffic) to the bottom of funnel (appointments, estimates, closed deals, revenue). Most agencies only report on leads. We report on revenue. Your dashboards will show: Leads ? Appointments ? Estimates Given ? Sold ? Revenue — broken down by marketing channel.

Multi-channel dashboards — Blending data from GA4, Google Ads, Google Business Profile,
 Ads, Google Search Console, and GoHighLevel CRM into one unified dashboard. Clients don't want to log into 6 platforms. They want one dashboard that shows everything.

GHL data integration — Connecting GoHighLevel CRM data into Looker Studio using connectors (
, Windsor.ai, or GHL's native connector). This lets us pull pipeline data (opportunities, stages, revenue) directly into our reports alongside marketing data.

Ad creative reporting — Using Looker Studio's IMAGE function to display actual ad creatives alongside their performance metrics (CTR, CPC, conversion rate). Clients love seeing WHICH ads are working, not just numbers.

Automated report delivery — Scheduling dashboards to auto
 to clients on a set cadence. No manual PDF exports. No "sorry the report is late."

Custom calculations and blended data — Calculated fields for ROAS, blended CPL across channels, lead-to-close ratios, and other custom KPIs specific to each client's goals.

White-labeled design — Clean, professional dashboards with our branding (or the client's). Consistent color schemes, clear labels, intuitive layouts. Not a wall of charts that makes someone's eyes glaze over.



Attribution & Lead Tracking — Connecting the Dots

This is where you separate yourself from every other "analytics person." You don't just track website events — you track the full journey from ad click to closed deal.

What this involves:



UTM strategy and governance — Building and enforcing a consistent UTM naming convention across all campaigns, all channels, all clients. utm_source=google&ut
dium=cpc&utm_campaign=hvac-repair-emergency&utm_content=ad-v2 — every link tagged, every campaign traceable.

GCLID / FBCLID capture — Ensuring click IDs from Google Ads and
 Ads are captured on form submissions and stored in the CRM (GoHighLevel). This is the bridge between online clicks and offline sales.

Offline conversion tracking — Working with our Google Ads specialist to push conversion events back to Google and
 when leads progress through the pipeline. When a lead that came from a Google Ad becomes a $12,000 roofing job, that data goes back to Google so the algorithm gets smarter.

WhatConverts integration (or equivalent) — Setting up and managing call tracking, form tracking, and chat tracking through WhatConverts. Attributing every single lead — phone call, form fill, chat, text — to the exact marketing source, medium, campaign, and keyword that generated it.

Multi-touch attribution modeling — Understanding and implementing attribution models beyond last-click. First-touch vs. last-touch vs. linear vs. time-decay vs. data-driven. You know which model tells the most honest story for home service businesses (spoiler: it's usually a combination) and you can explain the tradeoffs to both the team and clients.

Cross-channel attribution — A homeowner sees a
 ad, Googles the company name two days later, clicks an organic result, then calls from the website. Who gets credit? You know how to build attribution frameworks that capture this reality instead of pretending the last click did all the work.



GoHighLevel CRM Data & Reporting

Our agency runs on GoHighLevel. You'll need to work closely with our GHL specialist to ensure data flows cleanly between the CRM and our reporting stack.

Key responsibilities:



Custom field and property setup — Making sure the right data is captured on every contact and opportunity in GHL: lead source, campaign, keyword, service type, deal value, pipeline stage, appointment type (1-leg vs 2-leg), and outcome.

GHL ? Looker Studio pipelines — Setting up automated data flows from GHL into Looker Studio (via 
, Windsor.ai, or direct API connections) so pipeline and revenue data shows up in real-time dashboards alongside marketing data.

GHL ? BigQuery (future) — As we scale, we'll likely move to a BigQuery data warehouse. You should understand the concept and be ready to help architect that when the time comes.

GHL dashboard reporting — Building internal dashboards within GHL itself for quick operational views: today's leads, this week's appointments, pipeline value by stage, team performance.

Conversion event mapping — Mapping GHL pipeline stages to conversion events that get sent back to ad platforms (Google,
) via workflows. "Appointment Set" = conversion event. "Estimate Given" = conversion event. "Job Sold" = conversion event with value.





Who You Are

GA4 Power User — You know GA4 far beyond the surface. Event configuration, custom dimensions, explorations, audience builder, predictive metrics, DebugView, data API, BigQuery export. You've configured dozens of properties and you know all the ways GA4 can break — and how to prevent it.

GTM Architect — Clean containers, logical naming, custom HTML tags when needed, data layer specifications, server-side tagging setup, Consent Mode configuration. You don't just add tags — you build a measurement infrastructure that scales across dozens of clients.

Looker Studio Designer — You build dashboards that are both beautiful and functional. Blended data sources, calculated fields, interactive filters, drill-down pages, conditional formatting, image tables, chart annotations. Your reports don't just show data — they tell a story that makes clients say "wow, I can actually see what's working."

Attribution Obsessive — You understand that "where did this lead come from?" is the most important question in marketing, and you've built systems to answer it definitively. UTM governance, click ID capture, offline conversion loops, multi-touch models — you connect the digital world to real revenue.

First-Party Data Strategist — You understand that in 2026, third-party cookies are dead, browser tracking is degraded, and the agencies that win are the ones with clean first-party data strategies: server-side tagging, Customer Match lists, Enhanced Conversions, and CRM-based attribution.

Detail Maniac — You audit tracking implementations before assuming they work. You catch that one event parameter that's misspelled. You notice when conversion numbers in GA4 don't match Google Ads and you find out why. You don't publish a GTM container without testing every tag.

Translator — You can take complex data and explain it to a client who doesn't know what a UTM is. "Your HVAC emergency campaigns generated 28 booked jobs worth $67,000 last month at a cost of $3,200 — that's a 21x return." That's how you talk to clients.



Requirements



3+ years of hands-on experience with Google Analytics 4 (not Universal Analytics — real GA4 expertise)

Expert-level Google Tag Manager skills (container setup, custom event tracking, data layer, debugging)

Proven experience building Google Looker Studio dashboards for marketing clients

Experience with conversion tracking across Google Ads and
/Meta Ads

Strong understanding of UTM parameters, lead attribution, and marketing funnel tracking

Experience with call tracking platforms (WhatConverts, CallRail, or similar)

Understanding of server-side tagging concepts and Consent Mode v2

Strong English communication skills (written and spoken)

Reliable internet and professional remote work setup

Available full-time during US business hours (or significant overlap)



Bonus Points:



Experience with GoHighLevel CRM and its reporting/data export capabilities

Experience connecting GHL data to Looker Studio (
, Windsor.ai, or API)

Experience with offline conversion tracking (GCLID capture ? CRM ? Google Ads)

BigQuery experience or understanding of data warehouse concepts

Basic SQL knowledge for querying GA4 BigQuery exports

Python or Apps Script for data automation tasks

Google Analytics and/or Google Ads certifications

Experience with home service or local business marketing specifically

Experience with SEO reporting tools (Google Search Console, Ahrefs, SEMrush)

Knowledge of
/Meta Conversions API (server-side)





What We Offer



Full-time position with a growing agency where data actually drives decisions (not just PowerPoint slides)

Ownership of the entire analytics and reporting stack — you're not a junior analyst pulling someone else's reports. You're THE data person.

Work alongside our Google Ads specialist, GHL specialist, SEO team, and content creators — your tracking and reporting makes everyone else's work better

Direct access to the agency owner who values data and ROI above vanity metrics

Long-term role with growth potential as we add more clients and more complex reporting needs

Competitive salary based on experience





How to Apply

We can tell the difference between someone who configures GA4 and someone who just looks at it. Don't bluff this one.

Here's what we need:



A short intro — Who are you, and what makes you genuinely passionate about analytics and data? (2-3 sentences.)

Your analytics portfolio — Describe 1-2 analytics or reporting projects you're most proud of. What was the setup? What tools did you use? What did the client learn or gain from your work? Be specific about the tracking stack (GA4, GTM, Looker Studio, call tracking, CRM integration, etc.).

A Loom video or screenshots (STRONGLY preferred) — Walk us through either:



A Looker Studio dashboard you built (show us the data sources, the design, the calculated fields)

A GTM container you manage (show us the organization, the tags, the triggers)

A GA4 property you configured (show us the events, conversions, audiences, explorations)

Any combination of the above. This is the single best way to stand out.





A troubleshooting scenario — Answer this in 3-5 sentences: A client says "our Google Ads report shows 60 conversions last month, but our CRM only has 38 leads from Google. Why don't the numbers match?" Walk us through how you'd investigate and diagnose this discrepancy.

Your availability and expected salary — Be direct.



Applications without the Loom/screenshots (#3) or the troubleshooting scenario (#4) will be deprioritized. We need to see how you think, not just what you list on a resume.



Quick Reference: The Full Analytics Stack We Need Mastered

Here's the complete toolkit, so there's zero ambiguity about the level we're hiring at:

Google Analytics 4: Property setup, data streams, event tracking, custom dimensions/metrics, key events (conversions), explorations (funnel, path, free-form, cohort), audience builder, predictive metrics, anomaly detection, DebugView, data API, BigQuery export, cross-domain tracking, data filters, attribution settings.

Google Tag Manager: Container architecture, tag/trigger/variable configuration, custom event tracking, data layer (dataLayer.push()), custom HTML/JavaScript tags, regex triggers, lookup/regex tables, server-side GTM containers, Consent Mode v2 integration, cross-domain linker, GTM Preview/Debug mode, version control and workspace management.

Google Looker Studio: Multi-source dashboards (GA4, Google Ads,
 Ads, Search Console, GHL, WhatConverts), blended data, calculated fields, parameters, interactive filters, drill-down pages, chart annotations, conditional formatting, IMAGE function for ad creatives, scheduled
 delivery, white-label design, data freshness management.

Attribution & Tracking: UTM taxonomy design and governance, GCLID/FBCLID/WBRAID capture, offline conversion tracking, Enhanced Conversions, WhatConverts (or CallRail) setup, call tracking numbers and routing, multi-touch attribution models (first-touch, last-touch, linear, time-decay, data-driven), cross-channel attribution frameworks.

CRM & Integration Layer: GoHighLevel data export and reporting, GHL ? Looker Studio connectors (
, Windsor.ai), GHL ? Google Ads offline conversion workflows, pipeline stage ? conversion event mapping, custom field architecture for lead attribution.

Privacy & Compliance: Consent Mode v2, cookie consent banner integration, server-side tagging for privacy-first tracking, first-party data strategy, GDPR/CCPA awareness for data collection practices.



Local Legends Marketing — We help local businesses become the names everyone trusts. You'll be the one who proves — with data — that our marketing is the reason why.

Numbers don't lie. Show us yours.

title: Web Scraping / Data Extraction Specialist (Login-Based Platform)
description:
Hey,

Looking for someone sharp who can help extract data from a web platform.

At the 
nt, I’m running into an issue where:

• The platform shows hundreds of results (500+) 

• But I can only access or copy around 20–30 records at a time 

• There are multiple pages, but data isn’t easily exportable 

I need someone who can:

• Extract all available data, not just what’s visible on screen 

• Work with login-based platforms (authenticated sessions) 

• Handle pagination / lazy loading / dynamic content 

• Deliver clean data into Excel or Google Sheets 

________________________________________

What you’ll likely be working with:

• JavaScript-heavy web apps 

• Infinite scroll or paginated results 

• Browser DevTools / Network tab 

• API extraction (if available) 

• Or DOM scraping if needed 

________________________________________

Ideal experience:

• Web scraping tools (Python, Selenium, Puppeteer, etc.) 

• Experience extracting from platforms that don’t allow easy export 

• Understanding of XHR / API calls / JSON responses 

• Able to work efficiently without breaking the platform 

________________________________________

Deliverables:

• Full dataset (not partial pages) 

• Clean, structured format (CSV / Excel) 

• Ideally repeatable process if this becomes ongoing 

________________________________________

Notes:

• This is not a basic copy-paste job 

• Looking for someone who understands how to get around data limitations properly 

• If you’ve done similar work before, mention it briefly
